claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 19 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you're asking about Alex, who is 19 years old.

Since Alex is over 16, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

This would include things like:
- A-levels or similar qualifications
- Approved vocational training courses (up to level 3)
- T Levels
- Scottish Highers

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is doing his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 19 and doing A-levels, I need to gather a bit more information.

**When did Alex turn 19?** (Please provide the approximate date or month and year)

This is important because there are specific rules about how long Child Benefit can continue after a child turns 19 if they're in approved education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He turned 19 in March of this year.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. And one more important question about Alex's education:

**When is Alex due to finish his A-levels?** (Please provide the month and year, or let me know if he's expected to finish this academic year)

This is important because Child Benefit can continue after age 19 but only until the end of the relevant education period.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's expected to finish at the end of this academic year - so that would be around June or July this year.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you currently live in the UK, and is Alex living with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester actually. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex?** In other words, do you provide for his needs and support him financially (such as providing food, clothing, and a home)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I provide for all his needs - food, clothing, a roof over his head, everything he needs really.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. And one final question:

**Are you or Alex subject to immigration control, or are there any immigration restrictions that would prevent you from accessing public funds?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there are no immigration restrictions. We're both UK residents with no issues accessing public funds.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✓ **You meet the responsibility criteria** - You live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and you're responsible for him by providing for all his needs.

✓ **Alex meets the age and education requirements** - Although he's 19, he's still in approved education (A-levels), which means Child Benefit can continue.

**Important to note:** Child Benefit will continue until **Alex finishes his A-levels (around June/July) OR until 31 August of this year, whichever comes first**. After a young person turns 19, Child Benefit stops at the end of their education or on 31 August following their 19th birthday.

Since Alex turned 19 in March and is expected to finish his A-levels around June/July, your Child Benefit for him will stop at that point (when he completes his course).

If you haven't already claimed Child Benefit, you can apply online or by post. If you're already receiving it, it should continue automatically until Alex finishes his education or the end of August.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and turned 19 in March of this year. He is currently enrolled in approved education (A-levels) at sixth form college and is expected to finish in June/July of this year. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Importantly, Child Benefit stops on 31 August (the end of the relevant education or training period) following the young person's 19th birthday, OR when they finish their education or training - whichever comes first. Since Alex turned 19 in March and is continuing his A-levels until June/July of this year (within the same academic year), he remains eligible. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, the claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all his needs, and there are no immigration restrictions. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex until he completes his A-levels (around June/July) or until 31 August of this year, whichever comes first.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex meets the age and education criteria as he is 19 years old and still in approved education (A-levels). The claimant meets all the responsibility requirements, lives in the UK with Alex, and has no immigration restrictions. Child Benefit will continue until Alex finishes his A-levels (expected June/July) or until 31 August this year, whichever comes first.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Importantly, Child Benefit stops on 31 August (the end of the relevant education or training period) following the young person's 19th birthday, OR when they finish their education or training - whichever comes first. Since Alex turned 19 in March and is continuing his A-levels until June/July of this year (within the same academic year), he remains eligible. The claimant lives in the UK, Alex lives with them, the claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for all his needs, and there are no immigration restrictions. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex until he completes his A-levels (around June/July) or until 31 August of this year, whichever comes first.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. Alex meets the age and education criteria as he is 19 years old and still in approved education (A-levels). The claimant meets all the responsibility requirements, lives in the UK with Alex, and has no immigration restrictions. Child Benefit will continue until Alex finishes his A-levels (expected June/July) or until 31 August this year, whichever comes first.
